title: Migrate from subprocess to Claude Agent SDK
source: Anthropic Claude Agent SDK documentation and claude_agent_sdk Python package
description: The CTO Orchestrator currently shells out to `claude -p` via subprocess.run() for all agent delegation. Anthropic's Claude Agent SDK (claude_agent_sdk) provides a native Python API for building multi-agent systems with proper tool orchestration, streaming, structured outputs, and error handling — eliminating the need for subprocess management, stdout parsing, and environment variable hacks (like stripping CLAUDECODE).
proposed_changes: Create a shared `agent_client.py` module that wraps the Claude Agent SDK. Replace `delegate_to_agent()` in delegate.py, `summon_meeseeks()` in meeseeks.py, `claude_scan()` and `delegate_evolve()` in prometheus.py with SDK-based calls. The SDK supports tool definitions, system prompts, model selection, and timeout handling natively — removing the need for subprocess.run(), env stripping, and manual stdout/stderr parsing. Start with delegate.py as the pilot, then migrate other scripts.
